name: Pork Chops in Cream Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Season the pork chops with salt and pepper.
2. In a large skillet, brown the pork chops over medium-high heat until golden brown on both sides.
3. Remove the pork chops from the skillet and set aside.
4. In the same skillet, melt 2 tablespoons of butter or oil over medium heat.
5. Add the finely chopped onion and sauté until translucent.
6. Stir in 1/2 cup water, 2 tbsp flour, brown sugar, catsup, and minced garlic.
7. Gradually whisk in the beef bouillon cube dissolved in 1/4 cup water until combined.
8. Simmer the sauce for 30-40 minutes or until it thickens, stirring occasionally.
9. Return the pork chops to the skillet; reduce heat to low.
10. Pour the sauce over the pork chops and simmer for an additional 2-3 minutes.
11. Remove from heat and stir in the sour cream.
12. Serve hot with steamed vegetables on the side.
Makes 6 servings, approximately 230 calories per serving.
